web.title: Workspaces - Bun
web.description: bun install will install dependencies for all workspaces in the monorepo, de-duplicating packages if possible. If you only want to install dependencies for ...
web.markdown:
> ## Documentation Index
>
> Fetch the complete documentation index at: [/docs/llms.txt](https://bun.com/docs/llms.txt)
>
> Use this file to discover all available pages before exploring further.

[Skip to main content](https://bun.com/docs/pm/workspaces#content-area)

Bun supports [`workspaces`](https://docs.npmjs.com/cli/v9/using-npm/workspaces?v=true#description) in `package.json`. Workspaces let you develop complex software as a _monorepo_ consisting of several independent packages.It’s common for a monorepo to have the following structure:

File Tree

```
<root>
├── README.md
├── bun.lock
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── packages
    ├── pkg-a
    │   ├── index.ts
    │   ├── package.json
    │   └── tsconfig.json
    ├── pkg-b
    │   ├── index.ts
    │   ├── package.json
    │   └── tsconfig.json
    └── pkg-c
        ├── index.ts
        ├── package.json
        └── tsconfig.json
```

In the root `package.json`, the `"workspaces"` key is used to indicate which subdirectories should be considered packages/workspaces within the monorepo. It’s conventional to place all the workspace in a directory called `packages`.

package.json

```
{
  "name": "my-project",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "workspaces": ["packages/*"],
  "devDependencies": {
    "example-package-in-monorepo": "workspace:*"
  }
}
```

**Glob support** — Bun supports full glob syntax in `"workspaces"`, including negative patterns (e.g.
`!**/excluded/**`). See [here](https://bun.com/docs/runtime/glob#supported-glob-patterns) for a comprehensive list of supported syntax.

package.json

```
{
  "name": "my-project",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "workspaces": ["packages/**", "!packages/**/test/**", "!packages/**/template/**"]
}
```

Each workspace has it’s own `package.json`. When referencing other packages in the monorepo, semver or workspace protocols (e.g. `workspace:*`) can be used as the version field in your `package.json`.

packages/pkg-a/package.json

```
{
  "name": "pkg-a",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "dependencies": {
    "pkg-b": "workspace:*"
  }
}
```

`bun install` will install dependencies for all workspaces in the monorepo, de-duplicating packages if possible. If you only want to install dependencies for specific workspaces, you can use the `--filter` flag.

```
# Install dependencies for all workspaces starting with `pkg-` except for `pkg-c`
bun install --filter "pkg-*" --filter "!pkg-c"

# Paths can also be used. This is equivalent to the command above.
bun install --filter "./packages/pkg-*" --filter "!pkg-c" # or --filter "!./packages/pkg-c"
```

When publishing, `workspace:` versions are replaced by the package’s `package.json` version,

```
"workspace:*" -> "1.0.1"
"workspace:^" -> "^1.0.1"
"workspace:~" -> "~1.0.1"
```

Setting a specific version takes precedence over the package’s `package.json` version,

```
"workspace:1.0.2" -> "1.0.2" // Even if current version is 1.0.1
```

Workspaces have a couple major benefits.

- **Code can be split into logical parts.** If one package relies on another, add it as a dependency in `package.json`. If package `b` depends on `a`, `bun install` will install your local `packages/a` directory into `node_modules` instead of downloading it from the npm registry.
- **Dependencies can be de-duplicated.** If `a` and `b` share a common dependency, it will be _hoisted_ to the root `node_modules` directory. This reduces redundant disk usage and minimizes “dependency hell” issues associated with having multiple versions of a package installed simultaneously.
- **Run scripts in multiple packages.** You can use the [`--filter` flag](https://bun.com/docs/pm/filter) to run `package.json` scripts in multiple packages in your workspace, or `--workspaces` to run scripts across all workspaces.

## [​](https://bun.com/docs/pm/workspaces\#share-versions-with-catalogs)  Share versions with Catalogs

When many packages need the same dependency versions, catalogs let you define
those versions once in the root `package.json` and reference them from your
workspaces using the `catalog:` protocol. Updating the catalog automatically
updates every package that references it. See
[Catalogs](https://bun.com/docs/pm/catalogs) for details.

⚡️ **Speed** — Installs are fast, even for big monorepos. Bun installs the [Remix](https://github.com/remix-run/remix) monorepo in about `500ms` on Linux.

- 28x faster than `npm install`
- 12x faster than `yarn install` (v1)
- 8x faster than `pnpm install`

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

[Suggest edits](https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/edit/main/docs/pm/workspaces.mdx) [Raise issue](https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/issues/new?title=Issue%20on%20docs&body=Path:%20/pm/workspaces)

[bun info\\
\\
Previous](https://bun.com/docs/pm/cli/info) [Catalogs\\
\\
Next](https://bun.com/docs/pm/catalogs)

Ctrl+I

This leads to npm packages that do not work for anyone on install, e.g.
```
pnpm add @getlang/parser
 ERR_PNPM_WORKSPACE_PKG_NOT_FOUND  In : "@getlang/utils@workspace:0.0.7" is in the dependencies but no package named "@getlang/utils" is present in the workspace
```
I must be misunderstanding something here | BetaWas this translation helpful? [Give feedback.](https://github.com/changesets/changesets/discussions/1389#) All reactions [![@thejasonxie](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/34463193?s=60&v=4)](https://github.com/thejasonxie) Comment options # {{title}} Quote reply #### [thejasonxie](https://github.com/thejasonxie) [on Aug 1, 2024Aug 1, 2024](https://github.com/changesets/changesets/discussions/1389\#discussioncomment-10213902) | | | --- | | Anyways, I did the same using bun shell. I don't need to do any git restore since I'm running in this in GitHub actions
```
// prep-for-publish.ts
// usage: bun ./prep-for-publish.ts
import { $ } from 'bun';
const packages = (await $`ls packages`.text()).split('\n');
if (packages[packages.length - 1].length === 0) packages.pop();
for (const pkg of packages) {
const pkgJson = JSON.parse(await $`cat packages/${pkg}/package.json`.text());
console.log(`Preparing ${pkg} for publishing...`);
// check dependencies
const deps = Object.keys(pkgJson.dependencies || {});
for (const dep of deps) {
if (pkgJson.dependencies[dep].includes('workspace:')) {
pkgJson.dependencies[dep] = pkgJson.dependencies[dep].replace(
'workspace:*',
'latest',
);
}
}
// check devDependencies
const devDeps = Object.keys(pkgJson.devDependencies || {});
for (const devDep of devDeps) {
if (pkgJson.devDependencies[devDep].includes('workspace:')) {
pkgJson.devDependencies[devDep] = pkgJson.devDependencies[devDep].replace(
'workspace:*',
'latest',
);
}
}
await $`echo '${JSON.stringify(pkgJson, null, 2)}' > packages/${pkg}/package.json`;
}
``` | BetaWas this translation helpful? Maybe [@zkochan](https://github.com/zkochan) can provide some insight on the differences? | BetaWas this translation helpful? # Support publishing for bun workspaces\#15246

[New issue](https://github.com/login?return_to=https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/issues/15246)

Copy link

Copy link

Labels

[bun installSomething that relates to the npm-compatible client](https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/issues?q=state%3Aopen%20label%3A%22bun%20install%22) Something that relates to the npm-compatible client [enhancementNew feature or request](https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/issues?q=state%3Aopen%20label%3A%22enhancement%22) New feature or request

[![@EdAyers](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5064353?u=c438d652e71cea1008142a7899db4303d47c5357&v=4&size=80)](https://github.com/EdAyers)

## Description

[![@EdAyers](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5064353?u=c438d652e71cea1008142a7899db4303d47c5357&v=4&size=48)](https://github.com/EdAyers)

[EdAyers](https://github.com/EdAyers)

opened [on Nov 19, 2024on Nov 19, 2024](https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/issues/15246#issue-2672028837)

Last edited by EdAyers

Issue body actions

### What is the problem this feature would solve?

I have a monorepo with various javascript packages that depend on each other, and I need to publish them to my private github package repository.

I am using [bun workspaces](https://bun.sh/docs/install/workspaces).

The workflow is iterate through each package in `./packages` and do the following

1. use `git diff` to check whether the contents of the package have changed since origin/main
2. get the current version from package.json and jq
3. get the latest version on the package repository using `npm info ${PACKAGE_NAME} --json` (as far as I can tell this isn't implemented in bun yet)
4. assert that the current version is > latest version on the repository (and there is a git diff), erroring if it's not (or automatically bumping it)
5. publish the package with `bun publish`

### What is the feature you are proposing to solve the problem?

There are a few blockers that prevent this working smoothly in bun. I couldn't find them in the docs but it's possible I've just missed it.

#### Support `bun publish --filter '*'`

There is no `--filter` support for the `bun publish` command:

```
bun publish --filter "./packages/*" --dry-run
bun publish v1.1.31 (e448c4cc)
ENOENT: failed to read tarball: './packages/*'
```

I'm also not sure if it's possible to try pushing but don't fail if the package already exists. Eg yarn has a [`--tolerate-republish`](https://yarnpkg.com/cli/npm/publish) flag.

If this was implemented it might be possible to just delete my script and replace it with a one-liner.

#### Support listing the workspace package directories in topological order

currently I'm doing something like

```
for dir in ./packages/*/ ; do
    # checks mentioned above ...
    bun publish
done
```

I would really like this to run in topological order on the package dependencies. It would be great if there was as command `bun workspaces list --filter './packages/*'` that listed all the packages in topological order in a way where they can be fed directly into a bash for loop like this.

#### support `bun info <package> --json`

This seems to be a reserved command at the moment, maybe there is a different command that does the same thing as `npm info`.

### What alternatives have you considered?

It might be possible to do this more easily with a bun script rather than bash.

At the moment the alternative is I'm just doing this workflow with yarn instead, I'm currently doing a feasibility study on switching to bun and this is the primary blocker.

👍React with 👍5GenrikhFetischev, innocenzi, ngdangtu-vn, migueldamota and p0rsche

[![macalinao](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/401263?u=80a21e11b1fbc9421b5488fca9193c0f3d0db7dd&v=4&size=80)](https://github.com/macalinao)

### macalinao commented on Aug 17, 2025on Aug 17, 2025

[![@macalinao](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/401263?u=80a21e11b1fbc9421b5488fca9193c0f3d0db7dd&v=4&size=48)](https://github.com/macalinao)

[macalinao](https://github.com/macalinao)

[on Aug 17, 2025on Aug 17, 2025](https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/issues/15246#issuecomment-3194923733)

More actions

Hi all, I was able to get Bun workspaces working with changesets.

I wrote a blog post here on my website on how to do this: [https://ianm.com/posts/2025-08-18-setting-up-changesets-with-bun-workspaces](https://ianm.com/posts/2025-08-18-setting-up-changesets-with-bun-workspaces)

[![](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/347564?s=64&v=4)coderabbitai](https://github.com/apps/coderabbitai)

mentioned this [on Jan 29on Jan 29, 2026](https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/issues/15246#event-4523500496)

- [chore: use bun bootstrap-vue-next/bootstrap-vue-next#2995](https://github.com/bootstrap-vue-next/bootstrap-vue-next/pull/2995)


[![](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/7664106?s=64&u=adf33ebad17d60870c32310c97f179847815e5a2&v=4)jrusso1020](https://github.com/jrusso1020)

mentioned this [on Mar 26on Mar 26, 2026](https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/issues/15246#event-5295280297)

# [How do you configure dependent packages in a monorepo for publishing?](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/77739876/how-do-you-configure-dependent-packages-in-a-monorepo-for-publishing)

[Ask Question](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/ask)

Asked2 years, 5 months ago

Modified [2 years, 5 months ago](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/77739876/how-do-you-configure-dependent-packages-in-a-monorepo-for-publishing?lastactivity "2023-12-31 16:52:15Z")

Viewed
888 times


This question shows research effort; it is useful and clear

2

This question does not show any research effort; it is unclear or not useful

Save this question.

[Timeline](https://stackoverflow.com/posts/77739876/timeline)

Show activity on this post.

I have a monorepo (using Bun.js workspaces) that has two packages, one dependent on the other. In addition, both these packages will be published for others to use on NPM.

The problem is that when the packages are published, one won't work because although it is dependent on the other, it's `package.json` lists it as a workspace dependency and not a registry one, meaning others can't use it.

What's the best way to handle this situation? Should I have an automated script that replaces `workspace:*` in `package.json` with a version number on publish, or is there some way I can have it automatically defer to the registry when it's not available as a workspace package?

Here's a look at the `package.json`:

```json
{
  "name": "@stylecss/cli",
  "description": "This package provides a CLI for Style, a simple & practical CSS preprocessor.",
  "author": {
    "name": "Aarvin R."
  },
  "homepage": "https://stylecss.js.org/",
  "module": "src/cli.ts",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "type": "module",
  "dependencies": {
    "@stylecss/compiler": "workspace:*" // <<<<< Dependency that doesn't work when installing this package
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/bun": "latest"
  },
  "peerDependencies": {
    "typescript": "^5.0.0"
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/aarvinr/style.git",
    "directory": "packages/cli"
  },
  "license": "MIT"
}
```

web.title: Setting up Changesets with Bun Workspaces - Ian Macalinao
web.description: The action publishes all packages with resolved dependencies. The beauty is that what I test locally with workspace:* references is exactly what ...
web.markdown:
# Setting up Changesets with Bun Workspaces

August 18, 2025

by [Ian Macalinao](https://ianm.com/)

Changesets does not support Bun out of the box: it doesn't resolve workspaces or catalogs. This is a known issue ( [Bun incompatibility](https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/issues/16074), [workspace protocol support request](https://github.com/changesets/changesets/issues/1454), [unmerged PR for workspace support](https://github.com/changesets/changesets/pull/674)). Here's how to resolve it.

## [The Problem](https://ianm.com/posts/2025-08-18-setting-up-changesets-with-bun-workspaces\#the-problem)

When you try to publish packages with Changesets in a Bun monorepo, your published packages will contain unresolved `workspace:*` references:

```json
{
  "name": "@macalinao/eslint-config-react",
  "dependencies": {
    "@macalinao/eslint-config": "workspace:*"
  }
}
```

This breaks npm installations for anyone trying to use your packages. The workspace references that work perfectly in development don't get resolved to actual version numbers during publishing.

## [My Solution](https://ianm.com/posts/2025-08-18-setting-up-changesets-with-bun-workspaces\#my-solution)

I created custom scripts that ensure workspace references get resolved properly. You can see the full implementation in my [style-guide repository](https://github.com/macalinao/style-guide):

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "ci:version": "changeset version && bun update",
    "ci:publish": "for dir in packages/*; do (cd \"$dir\" && bun publish || true); done && changeset tag"
  }
}
```

### [Why I Added `bun update`](https://ianm.com/posts/2025-08-18-setting-up-changesets-with-bun-workspaces\#why-i-added-bun-update)

The key insight was adding `bun update` after `changeset version`. Here's what happens:

1. `changeset version` updates the version numbers in package.json files
2. `bun update` then fixes the bun.lockb file to properly resolve all `workspace:*` references to these new version numbers

Without `bun update`, the lockfile remains out of sync with the updated package versions. This causes `bun publish` to fail or publish packages with unresolved workspace references. The `bun update` command ensures that when `@macalinao/eslint-config` gets bumped to version 4.2.2, the lockfile is updated so that `bun publish` can properly resolve and replace `workspace:*` with `4.2.2` during publishing.

## [Why I Publish All Packages](https://ianm.com/posts/2025-08-18-setting-up-changesets-with-bun-workspaces\#why-i-publish-all-packages)

Instead of using `changeset publish`, I iterate through every package and publish them individually:

```bash
for dir in packages/*; do
  (cd "$dir" && bun publish || true)
done && changeset tag
```

I do this because:

1. It ensures all `workspace:*` references are resolved before publishing
2. The `|| true` means if one package fails (maybe it's already published), the others still get published
3. I get direct control over the publishing process using Bun's native publish command
4. After everything publishes, I create git tags with `changeset tag`

This approach prevents partial-publish disasters where some packages would fail and leave my monorepo in an inconsistent state.

## [GitHub Actions Setup](https://ianm.com/posts/2025-08-18-setting-up-changesets-with-bun-workspaces\#github-actions-setup)

The GitHub action is largely the same as the official documentation, utilizing the [changesets/action](https://github.com/changesets/action) GitHub action. Here's my workflow from [release.yml](https://github.com/macalinao/style-guide/blob/master/.github/workflows/release.yml):

```yaml
name: Release
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - master

jobs:
  version:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2

      - name: Setup npmrc
        run: echo "//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}" > .npmrc

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Build
        run: bun run build

      - name: Create and publish versions
        uses: changesets/action@v1
        with:
          version: bun run ci:version
          commit: "chore: update versions"
          title: "chore: update versions"
          publish: bun run ci:publish
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
```

The workflow:

1. Sets up Bun using `oven-sh/setup-bun@v2`
2. Creates an `.npmrc` with my npm auth token
3. Builds all packages before publishing
4. Uses the changesets action with my custom `ci:version` and `ci:publish` scripts

When I push changesets to master, this workflow creates a PR with version updates. When I merge that PR, it automatically publishes all packages to npm.

## [Configuration Details](https://ianm.com/posts/2025-08-18-setting-up-changesets-with-bun-workspaces\#configuration-details)

Each package needs proper publishing configuration to publish to npm:

```json
{
  "publishConfig": {
    "access": "public",
    "registry": "https://registry.npmjs.org/"
  }
}
```

A similar configuration works for private packages published on GitHub Packages:

```json
{
  "publishConfig": {
    "access": "restricted",
    "registry": "https://npm.pkg.github.com/"
  }
}
```

The workflow just uses `bun publish`, so you can use the same configuration you'd use for `yarn` or `pnpm`.

## [The Complete Workflow](https://ianm.com/posts/2025-08-18-setting-up-changesets-with-bun-workspaces\#the-complete-workflow)

Here's how I release packages now:

1. I make changes and create changesets with `bun changeset`
2. I push to master, which triggers the GitHub Action
3. The action creates a "Version Packages" PR
4. I merge the PR, which triggers another action run
5. The action publishes all packages with resolved dependencies

The beauty is that what I test locally with `workspace:*` references is exactly what gets published to npm, just with resolved version numbers.

## [Conclusion](https://ianm.com/posts/2025-08-18-setting-up-changesets-with-bun-workspaces\#conclusion)

This workaround has been working reliably for my monorepo. While I'm looking forward to first-class support ( [tracked here](https://github.com/oven-sh/bun/issues/16074)), this solution gets the job done today.

The key takeaway: **always run `bun update` after `changeset version`** to fix the lockfile, and **use `bun publish` directly** for each package. This combination ensures workspace references are properly resolved during publishing.

If you're struggling with the same issue, check out my [style-guide repo](https://github.com/macalinao/style-guide) for a working example. The approach has eliminated all the workspace reference issues I was experiencing, and my packages now publish reliably with properly resolved dependencies.

Thanks for reading! Have any questions, comments, or suggestions? Feel free to use the comment section below or email me at [blog@igm.pub](mailto:blog@igm.pub) and I'll do my best to respond.

Alternatively, you can view the source of the post [here](https://github.com/macalinao/ian-website/blob/master/content/posts/2025-08-18-setting-up-changesets-with-bun-workspaces.md) and send a pull request.
